tools.name: minerva_nametag_maker_nametag_generate
tools.description: Generate a name-tag PDF and return the bytes. (Install-time metadata; the backend supplies the authoritative schema at runtime.)

tools.name: minerva_nametag_maker_nametag_save
tools.description: Generate a name-tag PDF and write it to disk. (Install-time metadata; backend supplies the runtime schema.)

tools.name: minerva_nametag_maker_nametag_build_from_sheet
tools.description: Build a .mtags document from spreadsheet rows + a column mapping. (Install-time metadata; backend supplies the runtime schema.)

skills.title: Nametag Maker
skills.summary: Use when the user wants name tags / badges / lanyards from a roster — a list, a file/xlsx/csv, or info typed in chat. Drives the nametag-maker: data → Minerva spreadsheet → preview ONE draft → iterate via annotations → full batch. The LLM does the data entry; the panel is preview+annotate only.
skills.preconditions: The nametag_maker plugin is installed and RUNNING (minerva_plugin_list; start with minerva_plugin_start if not). Data may come from a file, a doc, or chat — there need NOT be an input file.
skills.outcome: A reviewed .mtags nametag project (the user's annotated-draft feedback addressed) and, on request, a print-ready PDF of the full roster.
skills.steps:
Key idea: the SPREADSHEET is the data home (you + the user edit it); the nametag panel is PREVIEW + ANNOTATE, never a data-entry form. Iterate as DRAFTS: one tag → review → full batch.

1. Ensure the plugin runs: minerva_plugin_list. If nametag_maker isn't RUNNING, minerva_plugin_start id="nametag_maker".

2. Get the data into a Minerva spreadsheet (NEVER hand-enter tag data in the panel):
   - File/xlsx/csv: minerva_create_spreadsheet_editor file_path="<abs>" (native import).
   - A doc, or info typed in chat (no input file): minerva_create_spreadsheet_editor (blank), then fill it with minerva_update_spreadsheet_data / minerva_add_spreadsheet_row using the standard column template (system prompt §2).

3. Read the sheet: minerva_get_spreadsheet_data editor_name="<sheet tab>". Convert its rows to rows_json: a JSON array string of {column: value} objects (one per person).

4. Build a SINGLE-tag DRAFT first: minerva_nametag_maker_nametag_build_from_sheet with:
   - rows_json = the JSON array string,
   - mapping = {"title":"Name","subtitle":"Subtitle","lines":[{"label":"Teacher","column":"Teacher"},{"label":"Group","column":"Group"}]} (label = what prints; column = where the value comes from; "" label = value-only line),
   - preview_first_only = true (preview renders just the FIRST tag; the .mtags still stores ALL rows),
   - out_path = "<abs>.mtags", plus layout (default "detailed"), image_side, back_mode, full_guides as needed.
   Then minerva_open_file path="<abs>.mtags" to show the draft.

5. REVIEW + ITERATE: ask the user to review and ANNOTATE the draft. Read feedback with minerva_annotations_list editor_name="<file>.mtags" — anchored_to reads "tag: <name>" so you know which tag/field, and summary has the note. Apply changes to the SHEET (values/columns), the mapping, or options; re-run build_from_sheet (same out_path). If the open tab doesn't refresh, minerva_close_editor (force) then minerva_open_file again.

6. FULL BATCH: when the draft is approved, re-run build_from_sheet with preview_first_only=false (all rows) and re-open → the full sheet shows; the user can still annotate.

7. PRINT-READY PDF (on request): minerva_nametag_maker_nametag_save path="<abs>.pdf" with the same rows + layout/options.

CALL BUDGET: ~2-4 calls to build the sheet, 1 per draft/iteration, 1 for the batch. Surface missing-data people rather than guessing.
skills.system_prompt:
§0 — Mental model (read FIRST)

The nametag-maker turns a roster into printable name tags. Two hard rules:
1. The user does NOT hand-enter tag data. YOU extract their data — a file, an xlsx/csv, an existing doc, or info they typed in chat — into a Minerva SPREADSHEET. That spreadsheet is the editable data home, for you and the user. The nametag panel is a PREVIEW + ANNOTATE surface only; never treat it as a data-entry form.
2. Iterate as DRAFTS, like a designer: render ONE tag, let the user review and ANNOTATE it, apply their feedback, THEN do the full batch. Don't dump the whole sheet first.

§1 — The pipeline
[source: file | xlsx | chat] → Minerva spreadsheet (standard columns, §2) → nametag_build_from_sheet (single draft) → user annotates → iterate → full batch → save PDF. The source varies; the home is always the spreadsheet. With no input file, just create a sheet and fill it from what the user typed.

§2 — Standard column template (a SUGGESTION — adapt freely)
Name (the big name) · Subtitle (second line: Cabin / Title / Company / Grade) · then one column per detail line (Teacher, Group, Role, Email…). Each detail column becomes a "Label: Value" line; blank cells are omitted. The column MAPPING you pass to build_from_sheet makes any layout work — rename/add/remove columns to fit the event (a camp roster, a conference, a classroom, a mixer with no second line are all just different columns + mapping).

§3 — Reading feedback
After you open a draft, the user marks the rendered tag. minerva_annotations_list returns each mark with anchored_to "tag: <name>" plus a one-line summary — so you know which tag and field they mean. Apply changes to the SHEET (or the mapping / options), rebuild, reopen. Keep iterations tight; if data is missing for some people, list them and ask rather than guessing.

§4 — Options & limits
- back_mode "same" = reversible (same on both sides); default backs are blank. full_guides=true draws full boxes; the default is corner-cut Avery 5395 marks.
- build_from_sheet is TEXT-only (no per-row photos). For tags with a logo or photos, use minerva_nametag_maker_nametag_generate / nametag_save with icon_path / images.
- After any plugin schema change the user must reconnect MCP (/mcp), or new tool args may be mis-passed.
